title: CEO Commentary on Loan Condition – As of December 31, 2026
content:
CAMPUS's loan portfolio remains actively managed and appropriately reserved, though overall loan balances grew slightly from the previous period. Total loans of $6,657,944 thousand remain concentrated in First Lien Real Estate ($1,878,223) and New Vehicle ($1,557,711), which continue to represent the primary sources of both earnings and credit risk. Delinquent loans declined slightly from the previous period.

Portfolio Composition
CAMPUS's loan portfolio continues to be primarily concentrated in First Lien Real Estate. As of December 31, 2026, New Vehicle totaled $1,557,711 (23.7%) and All Other Non-Commercial totaled $81 thousand (0.0%), together representing approximately 23.7% of the total portfolio.

Portfolio Composition
All Other Secured Non-Real Estate totaled $25,189 (3.8%), while Used Vehicle accounted for $1,42,564 (21.4%). Overall loan balances grew slightly from the previous period, reflecting continued balance-sheet expansion.

Delinquency & Credit Quality
Credit performance remains manageable despite modest delinquency levels within certain segments. Total delinquent loans declined slightly, representing a decrease from the previous period. The largest delinquency exposure remains in First Lien Real Estate and New Vehicle.

The concentration of delinquencies within First Lien Real Estate and New Vehicle remains consistent with the overall portfolio composition. Management continues to monitor delinquency migration trends and collection performance to limit future credit deterioration.
